html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: 3D Gaussian surface centered at the origin with variable sigma
color gradient on the surface representing heights from blue (low) to red (high)
x-axis labeled with ticks from -4 to 4
y-axis labeled with ticks from -4 to 4
z-axis representing the Gaussian amplitude with scale from 0 to 1.5
sigma annotation on the xz-plane showing the spatial extent of the Gaussian

interactions.label: Sigma
interactions.teaches: Adjusting sigma changes the spread of the Gaussian surface, showing how wider sigmas smooth the surface over a larger area.
Hovering reveals the numeric height at any point on the Gaussian surface, illustrating its continuous nature.

demo_steps.title: Initial Gaussian surface
demo_steps.narration: Here we see the Gaussian surface with sigma=1. Notice its symmetrical hill shape reaching a peak at the center. It is smooth and positively contoured, representing the distribution's continuous nature.

demo_steps.title: Increasing sigma widens the spread
demo_steps.narration: As we increase sigma to 2.5, observe how the Gaussian surface flattens and spreads out. A larger sigma means the surface influences a broader area and remains smooth.

demo_steps.title: Decreasing sigma narrows the peak
demo_steps.narration: Now, reducing sigma to 0.5 shows a sharp, narrow peak. The surface becomes taller at the center and affects a smaller area around it, illustrating the inverse relationship between peak height and spread.

plan.camera_suggestion: Positioned diagonally above the x-y plane at an angle to clearly show the Gaussian surface and both axes, with enough distance to capture the full extent of the surface when sigma is at its widest.

inline_constraints.default_view: must match the original image silhouette, size, label scale, and crop
inline_constraints.visible_ui: compact slider visible near an edge with no filled panel, not covering important geometry
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips and click popups only
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: infer the source camera angle/projection/zoom so the first rendered frame is a drop-in visual replacement

plan.notes: Use vertex displacement for the Gaussian surface in a grid mesh to ensure accurate peak shapes. Implement a smooth color gradient along the z-axis from blue at the base to red at the peak to match the original figure's appearance.

elements: 3D Gaussian surface plot
grid on the base plane
color gradient from blue at base to red at peak
x and y axes

interactions.label: Sigma
interactions.teaches: Adjusting sigma changes the spread of the Gaussian — higher values broaden the peak and decrease the slope.
Orbiting the view helps understand the 3D structure and symmetry around the peak.

demo_steps.title: Standard Gaussian with Σ=1
demo_steps.narration: Here is the 2D Gaussian surface with sigma set to 1. Notice how the peak forms a smooth tip at the center of the plot, decreasing symmetrically around it.

demo_steps.title: Increasing Sigma Broadens the Peak
demo_steps.narration: Increase sigma to 2. The central peak becomes wider and less steep. Larger sigma values cause the Gaussian to spread over a larger area.

demo_steps.title: Comparing Gaussian Shapes
demo_steps.narration: Now decrease sigma to 0.5. The peak narrows and steepens, focusing the surface's influence more tightly around the center.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Angled view slightly above and to the side, providing a clear perspective of the peak and spreading base.

elements: 3D Gaussian surface plot with solid surface
color gradient from red at peak to blue at base
grid on the base plane
x-axis with labels
y-axis with labels

interactions.label: Sigma
interactions.teaches: Adjusting sigma changes the spread of the Gaussian — higher values broaden the peak and decrease the slope.
Orbiting the view helps understand the 3D structure and symmetry around the peak.

demo_steps.title: Standard Gaussian with Σ=1
demo_steps.narration: Here is the 2D Gaussian surface with sigma set to 1. Notice the peak at the center in red, smoothly decreasing to blue at the base as it spreads out.

demo_steps.title: Increasing Sigma Broadens the Peak
demo_steps.narration: Increase sigma to 2. The surface peak broadens and the slope decreases, spreading red across a larger area. Larger sigma values diffuse the central concentration.

demo_steps.title: Narrowing Sigma Sharpens the Peak
demo_steps.narration: Now, decrease sigma to 0.5. The surface becomes steeper and narrower, focusing more red at the peak and concentrating the Gaussian's influence at the center.

plan.camera_suggestion: Angled view slightly above and to the side, providing a clear perspective of the peak and spreading base. Match the original color gradient and ensure axes are visible in initial view.
